title: Scan the Fortress
module_title: Advanced Port Scanning and Firewall Evasion
learning_objectives: Apply multiple firewall evasion techniques to bypass network defences
Execute comprehensive port discovery including high-range enumeration
Deploy NSE scripts for service-specific deep enumeration
Document and justify scanning methodology choices
key_topics: Nmap scan types (SYN, UDP, ACK, etc.)
Idle scan and other advanced methods
scenario: A government ministry in Gaborone has deployed firewalls, IDS, and rate limiting. Standard scans return mostly filtered ports. Use advanced Nmap techniques to enumerate what is behind the defences.

scenario_context.setting: Government agency network with robust defensive posture in Gaborone
scenario_context.challenge: Circumvent firewall rules and IDS detection to identify actual network topology
scenario_context.relevance: African government systems increasingly deploy sophisticated defences; requires technical ingenuity

subtasks.title: Baseline Scan
subtasks.description: Run a standard Nmap scan and document filtered results.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Execute basic SYN scan against target range. Document all 'filtered' ports. This establishes baseline and demonstrates need for evasion.
subtasks.evidence_required: Standard scan output with filtered port count documented

subtasks.title: Evasion Techniques
subtasks.description: Use at least 3 evasion techniques to bypass the firewall.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test 3+ techniques: packet fragmentation, decoys, slow timing, ACK scans, null scans, and FIN scans. Document which techniques yield results.
subtasks.evidence_required: Evasion scan outputs with technique explanations and results comparison

subtasks.title: Full Port Discovery
subtasks.description: Identify ALL open ports including high-range.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Once evasion technique succeeds, execute full-range scan (1-65535) or targeted high-port scans. Document all open/open-filtered ports.
subtasks.evidence_required: Complete port listing with baseline comparison showing improvement

subtasks.title: NSE Deep Enumeration
subtasks.description: Run targeted NSE scripts against every service.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: For each open port, run relevant NSE scripts for service enumeration, version detection, vulnerability discovery. Document findings per service.
subtasks.evidence_required: Annotated NSE output with key intelligence highlighted

subtasks.title: Scan Methodology Report
subtasks.description: Document what worked, what failed, and why.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Create detailed report explaining baseline results, each evasion technique attempted, success rates, and final methodology. Include timing analysis and stealth assessment.
subtasks.evidence_required: Comprehensive scan methodology report with analysis

resources.name: NSE Script Selection for Network Enumeration

reflection_prompt: Which evasion technique was most effective and why? How do you balance thoroughness against detection risk in a monitored environment?

quiz.question: Why are fragmentation-based evasion techniques effective against stateless firewalls?
options: Firewall rules can't reassemble packets before inspection
quiz.explanation: Stateless firewalls inspect individual packets without reassembly. Fragmented packets bypass rules checking on assembled data.

quiz.question: What is the primary advantage of ACK scans in firewall evasion?
options: Can determine open vs closed ports
Appears as part of established connection
quiz.explanation: ACK scans appear as responses to established connections, often bypassing stateful firewall rules that allow response traffic.

mentor_notes: Evaluate creativity in evasion technique selection and actual improvement achieved. Assessment should consider not just ports found, but quality of methodology documentation and understanding of why techniques work.
